I've played sub games since the Silent service days and have to say this one is horrible. I really wanted to like it and have walked away from it a couple times hoping they would fix the bugs of this game. Well after the 5th time of trying it again I'm giving up. The game looks beautiful but that amount of bugs and glitches make you give want to give up. I wouldn't even waste the $10 on the game.


*UPDATE*

I revisited this game once more after giving up on it. The only way I was even going to attempt to play this game was with the subsim mods installed (which I have never used until this point). My original view on the game still stands on the base game and would not recommend paying full price. Ubisoft shouldn't have released such a horrible product to WWII sub diehards. I recommend that if you do buy the game, run over to subsim.com and mod it to a much more enjoyable game.

Make sure you have the latest version of Uplay - and everything will work great. 
Install the Wolves of Steel mod.  Its a bit of a schlepp to do it. But its irons out lots of bugs and adds excellent realism levels so that you can choose how complex or simple you want your gameplay to be. Vanilla is OK, but with the Wolves of Steel mod its brilliant.  Choose whether you want Arcade where you just aim and fire,  or sitting in your lounge with a red bulb screwed into your desk lamp brushing up on your maritime German.  Its a fantastic game with a steep learning curve and hours of fun once you master it,   Love it.

If they at least had an option to stay with the submarine while it goes down or.. go back to the main menu.. That would be really cool but apart from that its a really good game!

I've played hundreds of hours of submarine sims. After reading and consulting the manual and giving SH5 the benefit of the doubt...I can't recommend it. Never have I rage-quit a sub-sim...until I played SH5, that is. Why would the devs make a game, such as SH5, that is the polar opposite to what they got right with Silent Hunter 3? The game just doesn't work for me. I wish it did, as I was really wanting to sink some ships, but couldn't find my Officer that was in charge of determining the firing solution of the torpedoes. He simply was not on the boat!

Such a huge difference from Silent Hunter 3 and Silent Hunter 4.  Graphically better and the controls are simple enough to sail your Submarine.  You can interact with your crew and skill/award them.  The whole feel to the game is very slick and polished.
The downside from steam point of view is that it wont play from just clicking the start button.  It activates the UBIsoft update server, which fails.  You have to download and register with Uplay in order to get it to work.  You also need to stay logged onto Uplay while you then play it from your Steam account.  The link is here for ease of use. https://uplay.ubisoft.com/en-GB

Very disappointed with Steam but the game is good after you fix it yourself.
Something to be aware of is this game in most cases WILL NOT PLAY after download from Steam without jumping through some hoops.  You will most likely have to fix the problems with this game yourself which is unsatisfactory but guess there's no way around that.  When I first purchased from Steam and tried to play I got the notorious "service not available" error and the game does not launch.  It appears that "Uplay" is the problem. at least it was for me.  To fix this problem I went to Ubisoft and downloaded the "OFFICIAL" updated version of Uplay.  I DO NOT RECOMMEND A 3RD PARTY site download but that's up to you.  Here is the legit Ubisoft link for the Uplay update to get Silent Hunter V to work:
https://uplay.ubisoft.com/en-GB.  After downloading and installing Uplay directly from Ubisoft the game works like it should on my 64-bit Windows 10 Pro computer with the latest windows updates.

This is something that Steam should know and take care of before taking your money and expecting you to figure things out on your own.  That is very unprofessional of Steam and makes them look very bad.  This is a widely experienced problem with Silent Service V and Steam should be all over this fix but they are not.
This worked for me but your mileage may vary.

For those of you who want to buy this game, but have seen all the reviews saying it doesn't work --[b]LISTEN UP[/b]--

This game DOES work, but you just need to install uPlay in order to make it run. That's it. There's no complex file editing to be done here. All in all, it's a good game albeit with a bit learning curve.

Game is dated but still offers one of the best uboat campaigns available in gaming. I recommend downloading and installing the  mod pack "The Wolves of Steel" available outside of steam (but still steam compatible).  Offers it all as far as WW2 sub warfare goes, and you can customize the difficulty you wanna play at. Want to navigate using the stars? done. Want to develop and use your own shooting solutions? done. Want to decode your incoming  orders via the Enigma machine? done. Does any of that sound like too much work? well, rest assured, you can always go with an easier setting and avoid all that.

In my humble opinion,

Great series. 

I have played Wolfpack (the original from like, the 90's), Sub Command, Janes Fleet Command, Silent Hunter II, Silent Hunter III, Silent Hunter IV,  Silent Hunter V, Command Modern Air Naval Operations and Command Modern Operations. 

This game is the culmination of decades of submarine gaming. Its beautiful, but very far from perfect. 

I can say for myself, the most appealing part of the game is the ability to walk freely while aboard the sub. That adds a level of realism not seen in earlier sub games, at least to my knowledge. 

I do wish SH5 was more in-depth and open world, with a wider selection of submarines types, multiple playable sides, and perhaps a SH6 with the Pacific campaign. 

Also would love to see what a cold war version and modern day version of Silent Hunter would look like. 

Its buggy and needs a PC with some guts, but I can run it on the boat anchor I've got and still have fun. 

There is a huge following of these and other naval PC games on www.subsim.com, if you havent visited their website, I'd highly recommend a look. (I don't work for them or get paid by them or anything like that at all, just a cool site of similar topics). 

With these games I spend countless hours playing and I'm always wishing for more.

Would not run at first.   Followed the directions others gave to get around the problem and it worked.  Need to run in Windows 7 compatibility mode, and check the magic box.    This is done by finding the SH5 executable file in your steam library and choosing properties (or something of the like). 

Gameplay is similar to others in the series, which I have always liked.   Focus is on fighting the ship rather than managing the crew, which again, I am okay with.  Graphics are surprisingly good for a game this old.   It has aged well in that regard.
With Wolves of Steel, this is the most comprehensive U-Boat sim on the market.

All the negative comments about the game isn't false, the standalone game is not good. Thanks to the mod community this game is playable. Even if I only have 40+ hours I am still learning and each kill is worth the initial time it takes to set up the wolves of steel mod. The only reason I give it a thumbs up is for the mod, not the game. The graphics are good.

very nice!

It is kind of a yes and a no from me. It is mostly an ok game, but compared to it's predecessors, it is obviously lacking. 

Silent Hunter 5 is beaten easily by 4th and especially the legendary 3rd game of the series when it games to gameplay and features. 

SH5 is plagued with bugs. In my playing time I have seen ships disappear in front of me, the holes from my torpedo hits disappear etc etc. UI items seem to regularly disappear or not work (I have the oxygen bug out the most), characters don't do what they are supposed to, this mainly applies to the watch officer who very often stays on the conning tower when you sink.

The looks of this game are great compared to 3 & 4, but that is to expected from a newer title. 

More merchant ship types should have been added. You are mainly sinking 3-5 types of merchant ships which makes identification way too easy and is just plain lazy by the devs. There are 2 freighters and 2 tankers. Just... lazy. There should be a lot more variety on merchant ships in this game, since the U-Boats sunk vessels built between the years 1900-1945. 

What is absolutely love about this game is the free first-person movement inside the submarine, it is a lot better than the system from previous games. BUT! This does NOT really justify having only Type VII U-Boats to available for the player. You could sail with the type VIIs, IXs and even with the Type XXI. Again, just plain lazy. 

The crew management has been simplified radically, personally I preferred the system from SH3 but I am not really against the "rpg" elements that have been tried to implement here, but again they seem kinda lazy and they do not have a lot of dept to them. I also miss earning medals and promotions to your crew, it was a nice part of SH3, I know you can do that in SH5 as well but again, it is just simplified extremely. 

The AI is also not much better than it was in earlier titles. 

Many of these issues can be fixed (sort of) with mods, but to such an extent that the mods feel essential to the game. Many of the mods I added were menus and commands and gameplay thingies that I feel like should be in the game in the first place.

Anyway, SH5 beats it's predecessors in looks alone, in almost everything else, SH3 and SH4 blow it out of the water. Compared to other sims however it is still an ok game though but In my personal opinion, a heavily modded SH3 or SH4 are way better game than any version of SH5.

Bought this game months ago and have enjoyed playing every minute of it. You gotta have the Wolves of Steel mod to make it next level but man is it worth it. 

I would say this, Silent Hunter 3 is definitely more flexible and offers more to do, but there's something to be said for the graphics and atmosphere of this game. I love it, 10/10 would recommend to anyone looking for a naval genre game.

This game gets so much hate. Download Wolves of Steel, and enjoy, without this megamod I get why some SH3 diehards disliked it. I transitioned myself, as the graphics and overall experince of SH3 just was starting to seem dated. With this installed its like a brand new game, I still play it to this day simply because no one else can get a subsim right. The graphics and overall experience are both better than SH3. Only issue I have is that the type VII is the only usable sub, I really wish they added at least the type IX for long range campaigns.

For those who have issues running this with the mod on Windows 10, there are plenty of resources our there to help. I had an issue with it crashing to desktop after loading into the game, and had it fixed within 30 mins of troubleshooting. Its not game breaking and taking some time is worth the hours of gameplay in this very immersive WWII subsim.

Truly a fun game! All you have to do is take 5 minutes before you play to download the uplay launcher from the ubisoft website (and then sign-in if you have an account), disable the High DPI adjustment under compatibility on the .exe file for Sh5, and then lastly type in the product code into the uplay store so it can properly link the game to your account. (If you cant find the code, right click on the game in your library and one of the options should say something about a code, but steam game me a popup message after running the game for the first time so i guess do whichever is quickest). Its working fine for me and the game has only crashed once (probably because of my low tier laptop). I hope this helped! Truly a fun and beautiful game.
